Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Responsive To Request For Production Nos. 8,9, And 20 Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatory Nos. 4,12,13,14,15,24,29,30 And 31
Off Calendar Jun-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Michael M.

APPEARANCES:
Michael M. Carlson and Wade Maxwell Rhyne for defendant and moving party: Lenscrafters Inc.
Matthew D. Davis for Plaintiff and opposing party: David Sandborg
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's Lenscrafter's motion to compel further responses to interrogatories and production of documents: granted
Request for production:
8, 9:
Plaintiff is ordered to produce all responsive documents or respond pursuant to CCP 2031 (g)(2).
20:
The objection is overruled and stricken. If any documents are withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege, plaintiff shall prepare a privilege log
Interrogatories
4, 10, 12, 13, 14, 24
The objection of 'premature' is overruled and stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further.
15, 29, 30, 31
The objections are overruled and stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further and state facts in support of the respective contentions.
Further responses are to be provided within 15 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Mtn For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Jun-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael M. Carlson and Wade Maxwell Rhyne for defendant and moving party: Lenscrafters Inc.
Matthew D. Davis for Plaintiff and opposing party: David Sandborg

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Lenscrafter's motion for a protective order: denied
While the inquiry regarding patient records and supervision of technicians is arguably less directly probative than questions relating to patient care, the Court does not find that the limited inquiry described in the motion establishes such undue expense, annoyance, and irrelevancy to the subject matter of the action to justify the prospective relief sought by defendant. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
